_comment: Root manifest for the Ex-Ditector (Self Footprint Audit Pro) Apify project. This is a descriptive manifest of the multi-actor system + the platform integrations this product wires in. It contains NO credentials and asserts NO live deployment; all usernames/ids/secrets are placeholders supplied by the operator at deploy time.
name: ex-ditector
title: Ex-Ditector — Self Footprint Audit Pro (compliant)
description: Audits a user's OWN public digital footprint, preserves public evidence involving themselves, and reduces compulsive checking (Closure Mode). The opposite of an ex-stalking tool. Every run is gated by shared/scope.js (scope_type: self | consented | public_figure | brand | safety_evidence).

discovery.role: A2 enumerate public sources for the vetted subject

crawler.role: A3 adaptive crawl + evidence capture, compliance backoff

report-builder.role: A6 compliant scoring + Closure Mode report

webhooks.capability: Apify run Webhooks with an OUTPUT-HEALTH receiver
webhooks.summary: Subscribes to ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED/FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED and verifies that a clean 'SUCCEEDED' actually produced real, compliant output before claiming an audit is ready. Treats all-backoff runs as a COMPLIANCE STOP (human review), empty/malformed output as NOT ready. Implements the 'success != valid output' rule against the product's NO-FAKE-DATA standard.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: read-only; lets the receiver fetch dataset/OUTPUT to judge health. Without it, health = UNKNOWN (never guessed up).
env.APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET: URL secret token; receiver verifies path /apify-webhook/<secret>.
env.APIFY_WEBHOOK_HMAC_SECRET: optional HMAC-SHA256 secret over the raw body (defense in depth).

schedules.capability: Apify Schedules (cron) for PACED, anti-compulsion re-audits
schedules.summary: Recurring re-audits driven by a fail-closed policy engine (cadence-policy.js) instead of a person hitting refresh. Embodies Closure Mode in cron: there is NO high-frequency option, and a higher distress_risk_score yields a SLOWER cadence, never faster. The effective interval is always the slower of (requested cadence, distress floor). Scheduling is restricted to scope=self|public_figure (a dual-use chokepoint, fail-closed); consented/brand/safety_evidence cannot be auto-scheduled. cron is always re-derived from policy, never trusted from config (Scrapy item-pipeline drop pattern). Schedule names carry only a short non-identifying subject_token (HIBP k-anonymity stance), never an email/handle/name.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: write token for POST /v2/schedules. Without it, register-schedules.js dry-runs and prints the exact body (never live).

exports.capability: Apify Dataset Views + marking-scoped Export API
exports.summary: Governs how audit data LEAVES the platform. Declares native dataset views (transformation+display in .actor/actor.json) and a real GET /v2/datasets/{id}/items export client (format/fields/omit/clean/flatten), both bound to a STIX/MISP-style distribution marking. Fail-closed redaction: raw locators (url/html_key/screenshot_key/note/subject_label) NEVER leave TLP:RED; shareable bands keep only content/html HASHES, coarse status, change flags and timestamps. The export fields/omit are DERIVED from policy, never trusted from the caller; an unknown marking is refused, not widened. Closure-Mode friendly: a shared summary proves THAT something changed without a clickable destination.

env.APIFY_TOKEN: read token for GET /v2/datasets/{id}/items. Without it, export-client.js DRY-RUNS and prints the exact request URL it would send (never live, never fabricated rows).

platform_constraints.tabular_limits: csv/xlsx/html capped at 2000 columns, column names <= 200 chars

compliance.notes: No private-individual tracking, no romance/intimacy/gender inference, no private-social scraping, no login/captcha/rate-limit evasion. Dual-use enumeration only for scope=self|public_figure via validateScope.
